records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What a Person Should Say When Performing Hajj or Tawaf on Behalf of Another
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2967 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a man performing Hajj on behalf of his brother, his father, or another person. Should he say anything specific?
Imam (as) said: "Yes, after entering into Ihram, he should say: 'O Allah (swt), whatever hardship, difficulty, trial, or dishevelment I experience during this journey, grant its reward to [the person on whose behalf the Hajj is performed], and reward me for fulfilling it on their behalf.'"
records.arabicText: 2967 - رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَقْضِي عَنْ أَخِيهِ أَوْ عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَوْ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنَ اَلنَّاسِ اَلْحَجَّ هَلْ يَنْبَغِي لَهُ أَنْ يَتَكَلَّمَ بِشَيْءٍ قَالَ «نَعَمْ يَقُولُ عِنْدَ إِحْرَامِهِ بَعْدَ مَا يُحْرِمُ اَللَّهُمَّ مَا أَصَابَنِي فِي سَفَرِي هَذَا مِنْ نَصَبٍ أَوْ شِدَّةٍ أَوْ بَلاَءٍ أَوْ شَعَثٍ فَأْجُرْ فُلاَناً فِيهِ وَ أْجُرْنِي فِي قَضَائِي عَنْهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2967 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a man performing Hajj on behalf of his brother, his father, or another person. Should he say anything specific?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, after entering into Ihram, he should say: 'O Allah (swt), whatever hardship, difficulty, trial, or dishevelment I experience during this journey, grant its reward to [the person on whose behalf the Hajj is performed], and reward me for fulfilling it on their behalf.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What a Person Should Say When Performing Hajj or Tawaf on Behalf of Another
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2968 - In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"If you wish to perform Tawaf around the Ka'bah on behalf of one of your brothers, go to the Black Stone and say: 'In the name of Allah (swt), O Allah (swt), accept this from [the name of the person].'"
records.arabicText: 2968 - وَفِي رِوَايَةِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ : إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَطُوفَ بِالْبَيْتِ عَنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ إِخْوَانِكَ، فَائْتِ الْحَجَرَ الْأَسْوَدَ وَقُلْ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنْ فُلَانٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2968 - In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you wish to perform Tawaf around the Ka'bah on behalf of one of your brothers, go to the Black Stone and say: 'In the name of Allah (swt), O Allah (swt), accept this from [the name of the person].'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What a Person Should Say When Performing Hajj or Tawaf on Behalf of Another
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2969 - It was narrated from al-Bazanti that a man asked Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (as) about a person performing Hajj on behalf of another. Should he mention that person's name explicitly?
Imam (as) replied: "Allah (swt), the Exalted, is not unaware of anything."
records.arabicText: 2969 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْبَزَنْطِيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحُجُّ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُسَمِّيهِ بِاسْمِهِ قَالَ «اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لاَ تَخْفَى عَلَيْهِ خَافِيَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2969 - It was narrated from al-Bazanti that a man asked Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (as) about a person performing Hajj on behalf of another. Should he mention that person's name explicitly?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "Allah (swt), the Exalted, is not unaware of anything."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What a Person Should Say When Performing Hajj or Tawaf on Behalf of Another
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2970 - It was narrated from Muthanna ibn Abdul-Salam, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a person performing Hajj on behalf of someone else. Should he mention that person’s name at every station?
Imam (as) said: "If he wishes, he may do so, and if he wishes, he may not. Allah (swt) knows that he has performed Hajj on their behalf. However, he should mention the person's name when offering the sacrifice (udhiyah) at the time of slaughter."
records.arabicText: 2970 - وَ رَوَى مُثَنَّى بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلسَّلاَمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَحُجُّ عَنِ اَلْإِنْسَانِ يَذْكُرُهُ فِي اَلْمَوَاطِنِ كُلِّهَا قَالَ «إِنْ شَاءَ فَعَلَ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ لَمْ يَفْعَلْ اَللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ قَدْ حَجَّ عَنْهُ وَ لَكِنْ يَذْكُرُهُ عِنْدَ اَلْأُضْحِيَّةِ إِذَا هُوَ ذَبَحَهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2970 - It was narrated from Muthanna ibn Abdul-Salam, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a person performing Hajj on behalf of someone else. Should he mention that person’s name at every station?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he wishes, he may do so, and if he wishes, he may not. Allah (swt) knows that he has performed Hajj on their behalf. However, he should mention the person's name when offering the sacrifice (udhiyah) at the time of slaughter."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Person Performing Hajj or Tawaf on Behalf of Another or Sharing a Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2971 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated: I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "My father has performed Hajj, my mother has performed Hajj, and my two brothers have also performed Hajj. I wish to include them in my Hajj, as I would love for them to be with me."
Imam (as) said: "Include them with you. Allah (swt), the Exalted, will grant them the reward of Hajj, and you will also have your Hajj, along with a reward for maintaining ties with them."
records.arabicText: 2971 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ أَبِي قَدْ حَجَّ وَ وَالِدَتِي قَدْ حَجَّتْ وَ إِنَّ أَخَوَيَّ قَدْ حَجَّا وَ قَدْ أَرَدْتُ أَنْ أُدْخِلَهُمْ فِي حَجَّتِي كَأَنِّي قَدْ أَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ يَكُونُوا مَعِي فَقَالَ «اِجْعَلْهُمْ مَعَكَ فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ جَاعِلٌ لَهُمْ حَجّاً وَ لَكَ حَجّاً وَ لَكَ أَجْراً بِصِلَتِكَ إِيَّاهُمْ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2971 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated: I said to Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"My father has performed Hajj, my mother has performed Hajj, and my two brothers have also performed Hajj. I wish to include them in my Hajj, as I would love for them to be with me."
Imam (as) said: "Include them with you. Allah (swt), the Exalted, will grant them the reward of Hajj, and you will also have your Hajj, along with a reward for maintaining ties with them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Person Performing Hajj or Tawaf on Behalf of Another or Sharing a Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2972 - Imam (as) said:
"Prayer, fasting, Hajj, charity, and emancipation (of slaves) reach the deceased in their grave."
records.arabicText: 2972 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «يَدْخُلُ عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ فِي قَبْرِهِ اَلصَّلاَةُ وَ اَلصَّوْمُ وَ اَلْحَجُّ وَ اَلصَّدَقَةُ وَ اَلْعِتْقُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Person Performing Hajj or Tawaf on Behalf of Another or Sharing a Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2973 - A man said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"May I be your ransom! I had intended to include my mother or some of my family in my Hajj this year, but I forgot."
Imam (as) replied: "Do it now; include them both."
records.arabicText: 2973 - وَ قَالَ رَجُلٌ لِلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ نَوَيْتُ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ فِي حَجَّتِي اَلْعَامَ أُمِّي أَوْ بَعْضَ أَهْلِي فَنَسِيتُ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «اَلْآنَ فَأَشْرِكْهُمَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2973 - A man said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"May I be your ransom! I had intended to include my mother or some of my family in my Hajj this year, but I forgot."
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do it now; include them both."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Departing Early to Mina Before the Day of Tarwiyah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2974 - It was narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
I said to Abu al-Hasan (as): "Can a person leave for Mina a day or two before the Day of Tarwiyah to avoid the crowd and congestion?"
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2974 - رُوِيَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَتَعَجَّلُ اَلرَّجُلُ قَبْلَ اَلتَّرْوِيَةِ بِيَوْمٍ أَوْ يَوْمَيْنِ مِنْ أَجْلِ اَلزِّحَامِ وَ ضِغَاطِ اَلنَّاسِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2974 - It was narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
I said to Abu al-Hasan (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Can a person leave for Mina a day or two before the Day of Tarwiyah to avoid the crowd and congestion?"
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Departing Early to Mina Before the Day of Tarwiyah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2975 - In another narration, Imam (as) said:
"A person should not leave earlier than three days in advance."
records.arabicText: 2975 - وَ قَالَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : «لاَ يَتَعَجَّلُ بِأَكْثَرَ مِنْ ثَلاَثَةِ أَيَّامٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2975 - In another narration, Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A person should not leave earlier than three days in advance."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Departing Early to Mina Before the Day of Tarwiyah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2976 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"It is incumbent upon the Imam to pray the Zuhr prayer in Mina, spend the night there, and remain until sunrise. Then, he should depart for Arafat."
records.arabicText: 2976 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «عَلَى اَلْإِمَامِ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ اَلظُّهْرَ بِمِنًى ثُمَّ يَبِيتَ بِهَا وَ يُصْبِحَ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ اَلشَّمْسُ ثُمَّ يَخْرُجَ إِلَى عَرَفَاتٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2976 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is incumbent upon the Imam to pray the Zuhr prayer in Mina, spend the night there, and remain until sunrise. Then, he should depart for Arafat."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Departing Early to Mina Before the Day of Tarwiyah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2977 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Ja'far (as):
"Did the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) pray the Zuhr prayer in Mina on the Day of Tarwiyah?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, and also the Fajr prayer on the Day of Arafah."
records.arabicText: 2977 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : هَلْ صَلَّى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلظُّهْرَ، بِمِنًى يَوْمَ اَلتَّرْوِيَةِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ اَلْغَدَاةَ يَوْمَ عَرَفَةَ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Boundaries of Mina, Arafat, and Muzdalifah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2978 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar and Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The boundary of Mina extends from Al-Aqabah to the Valley of Muhassir, and the boundary of Arafat extends from Al-Mazameen to the farthest point of the standing area (Mawqif)."
records.arabicText: 2978 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ وَ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «حَدُّ مِنًى مِنَ اَلْعَقَبَةِ إِلَى وَادِي مُحَسِّرٍ وَ حَدٌّ عَرَفَاتٍ مِنَ اَلْمَأْزِمَيْنِ إِلَى أَقْصَى اَلْمَوْقِفِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2978 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar and Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The boundary of Mina extends from Al-Aqabah to the Valley of Muhassir, and the boundary of Arafat extends from Al-Mazameen to the farthest point of the standing area (Mawqif)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Boundaries of Mina, Arafat, and Muzdalifah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2979 - Imam (as) said:
"The boundary of Arafat includes the Valley of 'Uranah, Thuwiyyah, Namirah, and Dhi al-Majaz, and extends behind the mountain to its rear."
records.arabicText: 2979 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «حَدُّ عَرَفَةَ مِنْ بَطْنِ عُرَنَةَ وَ ثَوِيَّةَ وَ نَمِرَةَ وَ ذِي اَلْمَجَازِ وَ خَلْفَ اَلْجَبَلِ مَوْقِفٌ إِلَى وَرَاءِ اَلْجَبَلِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2979 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The boundary of Arafat includes the Valley of 'Uranah, Thuwiyyah, Namirah, and Dhi al-Majaz, and extends behind the mountain to its rear."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Boundaries of Mina, Arafat, and Muzdalifah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2980 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) stood at Arafat on the left side of the mountain. People rushed to the place where the hooves of his camel had been, trying to stand beside it. He moved the camel away, but they repeated the same behavior.
He (sw) said: "O people, it is not the spot beneath the hooves of my camel that is the place of standing (Mawqif), but rather, all of this is a place of standing." He gestured with his hand and said: "All of Arafat is a place of standing. If only the area beneath the hooves of my camel were valid, it would not suffice for the people."
He (sw) did the same at Muzdalifah, stating that the whole area is a valid place of standing.
He advised: "If you see a gap, move forward and fill it with yourself and your mount, for Allah (swt) loves that these gaps are filled. Avoid standing on the ridges, and beware of Al-Araak, Namirah, the Valley of 'Uranah, Thuwiyyah, and Dhi al-Majaz, as these are not part of Arafat."
records.arabicText: 2980 - وَ وَقَفَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِعَرَفَةَ فِي مَيْسَرَةِ اَلْجَبَلِ فَجَعَلَ اَلنَّاسُ يَبْتَدِرُونَ أَخْفَافَ نَاقَتِهِ فَيَقِفُونَ إِلَى جَانِبِهَا فَنَحَّاهَا فَفَعَلُوا مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ «أَيُّهَا اَلنَّاسُ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مَوْضِعُ أَخْفَافِ نَاقَتِي بِالْمَوْقِفِ وَ لَكِنْ هَذَا كُلُّهُ مَوْقِفٌ» وَ أَشَارَ بِيَدِهِ وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ « عَرَفَةُ كُلُّهَا مَوْقِفٌ وَ لَوْ لَمْ يَكُنْ إِلاَّ مَا تَحْتَ خُفِّ نَاقَتِي لَمْ يَسَعِ اَلنَّاسَ ذَلِكَ» وَ فَعَلَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي اَلْمُزْدَلِفَةِ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ فَإِذَا رَأَيْتَ خَلَلاً فَتَقَدَّمْ فَسُدَّهُ بِنَفْسِكَ وَ رَاحِلَتِكَ فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى يُحِبُّ أَنْ تُسَدَّ تِلْكَ اَلْخِلاَلُ وَ اِنْتَقِلْ عَنِ اَلْهِضَابِ وَ اِتَّقِ اَلْأَرَاكَ وَ نَمِرَةَ وَ هِيَ بَطْنُ عُرَنَةَ وَ ثَوِيَّةَ وَ ذَا اَلْمَجَازِ فَإِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْ عَرَفَاتٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2980 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) stood at Arafat on the left side of the mountain. People rushed to the place where the hooves of his camel had been, trying to stand beside it. He moved the camel away, but they repeated the same behavior.
He (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"O people, it is not the spot beneath the hooves of my camel that is the place of standing (Mawqif), but rather, all of this is a place of standing." He gestured with his hand and said: "All of Arafat is a place of standing. If only the area beneath the hooves of my camel were valid, it would not suffice for the people."
He (sw) did the same at Muzdalifah, stating that the whole area is a valid place of standing.
He advised: "If you see a gap, move forward and fill it with yourself and your mount, for Allah (swt) loves that these gaps are filled. Avoid standing on the ridges, and beware of Al-Araak, Namirah, the Valley of 'Uranah, Thuwiyyah, and Dhi al-Majaz, as these are not part of Arafat."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Boundaries of Mina, Arafat, and Muzdalifah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2981 - In another narration, Imam (as) said:
"Those who stand under the Araak trees have no valid Hajj, as they are not within the designated standing area (Arafat)."
records.arabicText: 2981 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ قَالَ : «أَصْحَابُ اَلْأَرَاكِ لاَ حَجَّ لَهُمْ وَ هُمُ اَلَّذِينَ يَقِفُونَ تَحْتَ اَلْأَرَاكِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2981 - In another narration, Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Those who stand under the Araak trees have no valid Hajj, as they are not within the designated standing area (Arafat)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Boundaries of Mina, Arafat, and Muzdalifah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2982 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) stood at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah), and people rushed to the place where the hooves of his camel were.
He (sw) gestured with his hand while standing and said:
"I am standing here, but all of this is a valid place of standing (Mawqif)."
records.arabicText: 2982 - وَ وَقَفَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِجَمْعٍ فَجَعَلَ اَلنَّاسُ يَبْتَدِرُونَ أَخْفَافَ نَاقَتِهِ فَأَهْوَى بِيَدِهِ وَ هُوَ وَاقِفٌ فَقَالَ «إِنِّي وَقَفْتُ وَ كُلُّ هَذَا مَوْقِفٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2982 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) stood at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah), and people rushed to the place where the hooves of his camel were.
He (sw) gestured with his hand while standing and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I am standing here, but all of this is a valid place of standing (Mawqif)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Boundaries of Mina, Arafat, and Muzdalifah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2983 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"My father (as) used to stand at Al-Mash'ar al-Haram where he would spend the night."
records.arabicText: 2983 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقِفُ بِالْمَشْعَرِ اَلْحَرَامِ حَيْثُ يَبِيتُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2983 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) used to stand at Al-Mash'ar al-Haram where he would spend the night."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Shortening the Prayer on the Way to Arafat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2984 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "The people of Mecca pray the full (four-unit) prayer at Arafat."
Imam (as) said: "Woe to them, or alas for them! What journey is more arduous than this? It should not be performed in full (but shortened to two units)."
records.arabicText: 2984 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ يُتِمُّونَ اَلصَّلاَةَ بِعَرَفَاتٍ فَقَالَ «وَيْلَهُمْ أَوْ وَيْحَهُمْ وَ أَيُّ سَفَرٍ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُ لاَ يُتَمُّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2984 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The people of Mecca pray the full (four-unit) prayer at Arafat."
Imam (as) said: "Woe to them, or alas for them! What journey is more arduous than this? It should not be performed in full (but shortened to two units)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Name of the Mountain Where People Stand at Arafah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2985 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked:
"What is the name of the mountain at Arafat where people stand?"
Imam (as) replied: "Al-Alal."
records.arabicText: 2985 - سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : مَا اِسْمُ جَبَلِ عَرَفَةَ اَلَّذِي يَقِفُ عَلَيْهِ اَلنَّاسُ فَقَالَ « أَلاَلٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2985 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked:
"What is the name of the mountain at Arafat where people stand?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Al-Alal."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Dislike of Staying at Al-mash'ar After Leaving Arafah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2986 - Aban narrated from Abdul-Rahman ibn A'yan, from Abu Ja'far (as):
He disliked staying at Al-Mash'ar after departing from it.
records.arabicText: 2986 - رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُقِيمَ عِنْدَ اَلْمَشْعَرِ بَعْدَ اَلْإِفَاضَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2986 - Aban narrated from Abdul-Rahman ibn A'yan, from Abu Ja'far (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: He disliked staying at Al-Mash'ar after departing from it.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Ritual of Walking Through the Valley of Muhassir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2987 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"When you pass through the Valley of Muhassir—a significant valley between Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) and Mina, closer to Mina—hasten through it until you have crossed it.
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) quickened his camel's pace in this valley and said:
'O Allah (swt), secure my covenant, accept my repentance, answer my supplication, and bless those I have left behind with goodness after me.'"
records.arabicText: 2987 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا مَرَرْتَ بِوَادِي مُحَسِّرٍ وَ هُوَ وَادٍ عَظِيمٌ بَيْنَ جَمْعٍ وَ مِنًى وَ هُوَ إِلَى مِنًى أَقْرَبُ فَاسْعَ فِيهِ حَتَّى تُجَاوِزَهُ فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ حَرَّكَ نَاقَتَهُ فِيهِ وَ قَالَ « اَللَّهُمَّ سَلِّمْ عَهْدِي وَ اِقْبَلْ تَوْبَتِي وَ أَجِبْ دَعْوَتِي وَ اُخْلُفْنِي بِخَيْرٍ فِيمَنْ تَرَكْتُ بَعْدِي.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2987 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When you pass through the Valley of Muhassir—a significant valley between Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) and Mina, closer to Mina—hasten through it until you have crossed it.
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) quickened his camel's pace in this valley and said:
'O Allah (swt), secure my covenant, accept my repentance, answer my supplication, and bless those I have left behind with goodness after me.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Ritual of Walking Through the Valley of Muhassir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2988 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il narrated from Abu al-Hasan (as), who said:
"The hastening in the Valley of Muhassir is a distance of one hundred steps."
records.arabicText: 2988 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْحَرَكَةُ فِي وَادِي مُحَسِّرٍ مِائَةُ خُطْوَةٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2988 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il narrated from Abu al-Hasan (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The hastening in the Valley of Muhassir is a distance of one hundred steps."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Ritual of Walking Through the Valley of Muhassir
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2989 - In another narration, it is mentioned: "A distance of one hundred cubits."
records.arabicText: 2989 - وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ : «مِائَةُ ذِرَاعٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2989 - In another narration, it is mentioned:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A distance of one hundred cubits."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Miss Standing at Al-mash'ar
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2990 - In a narration from Ali ibn Ri'ab, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever departs from Arafat with the people but does not stay with them at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) and deliberately or out of disregard proceeds directly to Mina, must offer a badnah (a camel as expiation)."
records.arabicText: 2990 -فِي رِوَايَةِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ أَنَّ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ أَفَاضَ مِنْ عَرَفَاتٍ مَعَ اَلنَّاسِ فَلَمْ يَلْبَثْ مَعَهُمْ بِجَمْعٍ وَ مَضَى إِلَى مِنًى مُتَعَمِّداً أَوْ مُسْتَخِفّاً فَعَلَيْهِ بَدَنَةٌ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2990 - In a narration from Ali ibn Ri'ab, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever departs from Arafat with the people but does not stay with them at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) and deliberately or out of disregard proceeds directly to Mina, must offer a badnah (a camel as expiation)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Miss Standing at Al-mash'ar
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2991 - Yunus ibn Ya'qub narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
I said to him: "A man departed from Arafat, passed by Al-Mash'ar (Muzdalifah), but did not stop there until he reached Mina, where he threw the pebbles at the Jamarah. He did not realize this mistake until the day had advanced."
Imam (as) said: "He should return to Al-Mash'ar, stand there, and then return to throw the pebbles at the Jamarah."
records.arabicText: 2991 - وَ رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ أَفَاضَ مِنْ عَرَفَاتٍ فَمَرَّ بِالْمَشْعَرِ فَلَمْ يَقِفْ حَتَّى اِنْتَهَى إِلَى مِنًى فَرَمَى اَلْجَمْرَةَ وَ لَمْ يَعْلَمْ حَتَّى اِرْتَفَعَ اَلنَّهَارُ قَالَ «يَرْجِعُ إِلَى اَلْمَشْعَرِ فَيَقِفُ ثُمَّ يَرْمِي اَلْجَمْرَةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2991 - Yunus ibn Ya'qub narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to him: "A man departed from Arafat, passed by Al-Mash'ar (Muzdalifah), but did not stop there until he reached Mina, where he threw the pebbles at the Jamarah. He did not realize this mistake until the day had advanced."
Imam (as) said: "He should return to Al-Mash'ar, stand there, and then return to throw the pebbles at the Jamarah."
